input.title: On-Demand Ride-Sharing Platform
input.description: A robust, high-availability platform connecting independent drivers with riders seeking transportation. The system encompasses distinct mobile applications for Riders and Drivers, alongside a centralized web-based Administrative Dashboard. Key features include real-time geolocation tracking, an automated dispatch and routing engine, dynamic surge pricing, and secure payment processing.

user_classes.Rider: A consumer who requests rides, configures pickup/drop-off locations, manages payment methods, and leaves driver ratings.
user_classes.Driver: An independent contractor providing rides, subjected to background checks, viewing heatmaps of demand, and tracking earnings.
user_classes.System Admin: A technical or operational leader managing pricing variables, viewing global telemetry data, and suspending fraudulent accounts.
user_classes.Customer Support Representative: An authorized staff member handling lost-and-found claims, resolving fare disputes, and reviewing SOS/emergency alerts.

definitions.ETA: Estimated Time of Arrival — predictive metric mapping real-time traffic conditions.
definitions.Surge Pricing: A dynamic pricing multiplier triggered by algorithms detecting when local rider demand outpaces available driver supply.
definitions.Dispatch Engine: The backend microservice responsible for geographically matching a ride request to the optimal active driver.
definitions.Geofencing: Virtual perimeters around real-world geographic areas used to trigger specific regional pricing rules or airport queueing logic.

scope: Cross-platform mobile applications for both Riders and Drivers natively parsing GPS hardware
Real-time WebSocket or gRPC communication maintaining sub-second vehicle location updates
Automated background check integrations for new driver onboarding pipelines
Cashless payment execution including temporary pre-authorization holds to verify funds
Dynamic pricing algorithm calculating time, distance, and localized demand metrics
Masked VoIP and embedded chat for Rider-Driver coordination without exposing raw phone numbers
out_of_scope: Shared/Pool ride permutations (multiple distinct riders sharing one vehicle route) in the initial v1 MVP
In-house mapping data rendering (platform heavily relies on Google Maps integration)
Bicycle, scooter, or food delivery verticals
constraints: The Geolocation tracking microservice must utilize Go (Golang) and Redis streams to efficiently handle the massive volume of concurrent coordinate pings
Driver location telemetry must update at minimum every 3 seconds while 'Online'
Platform must retain raw telemetry logs for 7 years conforming to general transportation liability regulations

operating_environment.frontend: React Native for iOS and Android mobile apps. React (Web) for the administrative console.
operating_environment.backend: A polyglot microservices architecture (Node.js/TypeScript for general business logic, Golang for core dispatch/telemetry) hosted on Kubernetes.
operating_environment.database: PostgreSQL with PostGIS extension for relational/spatial constraints, Redis for ephemeral caching, and Kafka for event streaming.

FR-01.title: Dual-App Authentication
requirements: Users register using phone numbers verified via SMS OTP
The system manages entirely separate access tokens preventing a Rider from logging into the Driver app without completing Driver onboarding

FR-02.title: Rider Profile Management
requirements: Riders can save Home/Work addresses, add multiple credit cards natively via Stripe, and customize accessibility preferences
Profile aggregates a lifetime rating average based on past driver feedback

FR-03.title: Driver Onboarding & Document Verification
requirements: Drivers must upload images of their Driving License, Vehicle Registration, and Proof of Insurance
System triggers an asynchronous background check via third-party integration (e.g., Checkr)
Driver accounts remain strictly 'Pending' and blocked from 'Going Online' until Admin/API approval

FR-04.title: Geolocation Telemetry Ingestion
requirements: Drivers' mobile apps continuously ping current Latitude/Longitude, Heading, and Speed exclusively when toggled 'Online'
Telemetry data routes directly to a Redis geohash memory structure allowing spatial queries under 10ms

FR-05.title: Ride Requisition & Fare Estimation
requirements: Rider selects a precise drop-off and pickup pin using mapping UI
System queries the Google Distance Matrix API capturing route metrics, returning an exact upfront price quote based on base fares, time, and distance parameters

FR-06.title: Vehicle Categorization Options
requirements: Riders select a vehicle class (e.g., Standard, Premium, XL) before booking
The fare estimation strictly adjusts multipliers based on the elected vehicle class

FR-07.title: Dynamic Surge Pricing Algorithm
requirements: Backend chron-jobs continuously compare regional driver supply vs rider demand within specific H3 hexagonal grid cells
If demand outpaces supply beyond a set threshold, the grid receives a multiplier (e.g., 1.5x) visually rendered as a heatmap for drivers

FR-08.title: Real-Time Dispatch Matching Engine
requirements: Upon confirmation, the system algorithmically identifies the nearest available driver meeting the vehicle class criteria using spatial querying
Dispatch engine cascades requests outward radially if the initial driver rejects or times out after 10 seconds

FR-09.title: Pre-Authorization Payment Mechanics
requirements: System temporarily holds (authorizes without capturing) the estimated fare amount on the Rider's credit card immediately when dispatch initiates
If the authorization fails (NSF), the dispatch is cancelled natively prompting the rider to update payment methods

FR-10.title: Ride Status & Live Routing Sync
requirements: Rider app visualizes the specific driver's vehicle icon moving smoothly across the map predicting exact ETA
Ride states strictly transition through: 'Accepted', 'Arrived', 'In Transit', and 'Completed'

FR-11.title: Masked Communications
requirements: Riders and Drivers can communicate via in-app chat executing via WebSockets
Twilio integration provides explicit anonymous VoIP proxy phone numbers allowing calls without exposing native PII

FR-12.title: Trip Execution & Navigation
requirements: Once 'In Transit', the Driver app natively overrides integrating directly with OS-level maps (Google Maps / Waze) sending turn-by-turn coordinate intents
Driver must explicitly swipe a UI component to officially conclude the trip

FR-13.title: Fare Capturing & Split Distribution
requirements: Upon 'Completed', the backend captures the formal Stripe charge modifying the amount if significant route deviations occurred
Platform automatically deducts its percentage commission, directly crediting the remainder to the Driver's internal digital ledger

FR-14.title: Post-Ride Rating System
requirements: Both Rider and Driver are universally prompted to rate the other (1 to 5 Stars)
Ratings under 3 Stars organically prompt a secondary UI allowing specific tagged feedback (e.g., 'Reckless Driving', 'Disrespectful')

FR-15.title: Driver Earnings & Payout Routines
requirements: Driver dashboard breaks down earnings daily, weekly, and by individual trip
Automated ACH clearing distributes balances uniquely to driver bank accounts weekly via Stripe Connect Custom

FR-16.title: Safety & SOS Integration
requirements: A persistent 'SOS' shield icon exists natively on both Rider and Driver screens during active trips
Actioning SOS immediately opens a line to local emergency services and blasts live tracking links to predefined 'Trusted Contacts'

FR-17.title: Promotions & Referral Coding
requirements: Riders can manually input promotional code strings verifying natively against PostgreSQL discounting explicitly the final capture amounts
Referrals generate automated account credits safely upon the invitee's first completed ride

FR-18.title: Airport Geofencing Queues
requirements: Major transportation hubs feature custom polygons drawing 'Virtual Waiting Lots'
Drivers entering the polygon join a strict FIFO (First In, First Out) queue overriding generalized radial nearest-match logic entirely natively

FR-19.title: Lost and Found Ticketing
requirements: Riders navigate a support tree explicitly reporting lost items bounding natively to specific historical trip records
Drivers receive specialized push alerts and internal inbox messages resolving disputes natively isolating personal contact natively

FR-20.title: Driver Heatmaps & Insights
requirements: Driver map visually portrays explicit colored polygons signaling generalized surge magnitudes natively helping independent contractors organically migrate toward profitable zones natively

FR-21.title: Admin Live Telemetry Dashboard
FR-21.description: Admins require a 'God View' visual interface rendering all active drivers natively across a city simultaneously evaluating generalized server health metrics smoothly
requirements: Displays aggregated real-time visualizations natively mapping driver velocity preventing server congestion safely

FR-22.title: Fraud Detection Engine
requirements: Heuristic scripts identify impossible GPS jumps (spoofing) suspending offending accounts implicitly
Riders initiating excessive rapid cancellation behaviors incur automated warning natively assessing minor cancellation fees explicitly

NFR-01.title: High Concurrency Execution
requirements: The Telemetry ingestion endpoints correctly handle 10,000+ RPS (Requests per Second) using gRPC/Golang uniquely minimizing latency constraints safely
Databases inherently favor eventual consistency natively broadcasting Kafka messages explicitly maintaining pure uptime organically

NFR-02.title: Offline Resilience
requirements: Driver applications caching active trip states gracefully resuming inherently resolving correctly mapping changes completely bridging brief cellular dead zones heavily
